Both new evals are highly discriminating: +0.82 pass rate delta (100% with skill vs ~18% without).
Without the skill, Claude defaults to Python/Perl/grep/mv for both tasks — technically capable but wrong tooling for Obsidian.
The sole passing assertion in both without-skill runs was 'provides a loop pattern' — Claude knows how to script, just not with the Obsidian CLI.
With-skill runs are actually ~5.5s faster on average despite reading the skill, likely because the skill gives direct answers without exploratory shell scripting.
These two evals, combined with eval 2 from iteration 1, form a strong CLI-knowledge test suite for the skill.
